opportunities.title: Mookerji Innovation Fund (MIF)
opportunities.description: Funding for BITSian founders at MVP/pre-revenue stage, with preferences for DeepTech and women-led startups.
opportunities.eligibility: At least one BITSian co-founder must be part of the startup team. The Mookerji Innovation Fund is exclusively for BITSian-led ventures, leveraging the entrepreneurial ecosystem and talent pool within the BITS Pilani community. Eligible BITSian co-founders include current students enrolled at any of the four BITS Pilani campuses (Pilani, Goa,… At least one BITSian co-founder must be part of the startup team. Eligible BITSian co-founders include current students enrolled at any of the four BITS Pilani campuses (Pilani, Goa, Hyderabad, or Dubai), or alumni who have graduated from BITS Pilani within the last five years. Startups must be incorporated as a Private Limited company. Other entity types (LLP, partnership, proprietorship) are not eligible. Startups should be at the MVP or pre-revenue stage. Preference is given to revenue-generating startups. For the ₹20 lakh tier (without co-investment), startups must not have received any prior funding from PIEDS. Startups previously funded by PIEDS may still apply for the ₹50 lakh tier with external co-investment. The investment is equity-based and structured at the time of disbursement. Student FounderCollege Affiliation RequiredPrior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: Offers ₹5 Lakhs – ₹50 Lakhs as seed funding. Amount₹5 Lakhs – ₹50 LakhsFund TypeSeed FundingRepaymentNon-RepayableEquity investment ranging from ₹5 lakh to ₹50 lakh — up to ₹50 lakh with co-investment from another investor and up to ₹20 lakh without co-investment. Beyond capital, founders receive coaching and mentorship from Silicon Road Ventures, PIEDS, and partner organisations, access to a community of BITSian builders, and a bridge to the US market for select companies. Standout ventures are eligible for follow-on institutional cheques of $250K to $5M from Silicon Road Ventures. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipNetworkingMarket AccessIncubationDisbursementMilestone Based
opportunities.timeline: The application process for the Mookerji Innovation Fund is designed to identify high-potential BITSian-led startups through a structured yet streamlined approach. Prospective applicants are required to submit an online application via the PIEDS platform, detailing their team, product or service, market opportunity, business model, and financial projections. Following an initial…The application process for the Mookerji Innovation Fund is designed to identify high-potential BITSian-led startups through a structured yet streamlined approach. Following an initial screening phase, shortlisted teams will be invited for a series of virtual or in-person interactions, which may include pitch presentations to the MIF investment committee and deeper dives into their technology and business strategy. Successful applicants will then proceed to a due diligence phase, where legal and financial aspects of the startup are thoroughly reviewed. Upon satisfactory completion of due diligence, an investment agreement will be formalized, leading to the disbursement of funds and the commencement of the comprehensive support program. Throughout the process, applicants are encouraged to demonstrate innovation, market potential, and a clear vision for scalability. Selection ProcessApplications are submitted via the PIEDS Accubate form. The PIEDS team and Silicon Road Ventures evaluate submissions, prioritising MVP and pre-revenue startups with at least one BITSian co-founder. Preference is given to revenue-generating ventures and startups in DeepTech, women-led, student-led, and high-risk moonshot themes. Selected startups receive equity investment between ₹5 lakh and ₹50 lakh, with the higher band typically requiring co-investment from another investor. Contact InformationEmail:[email protected]
opportunities.about: PIEDS, the Pilani Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Society, is BITS Pilani's dedicated Technology Business Incubator, serving as a cornerstone for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship within the BITSian community. With a strong track record of supporting nascent ventures and facilitating their growth, PIEDS leverages the vast intellectual capital and entrepreneurial spirit of BITS Pilani students and alumni. Its mandate is to provide a comprehensive ecosystem that transforms groundbreaking ideas into viable businesses, thereby contributing to India's startup landscape and technological advancement. The Mookerji Innovation Fund (MIF) is a significant initiative led by PIEDS, BITS Pilani, made possible by a generous endowment from Mr. Sid Mookerji. Mr. Mookerji, an alumnus and successful global tech entrepreneur, established this fund to strategically empower founders affiliated with BITS Pilani, providing essential capital, expert mentorship, and a supportive community of innovators to facilitate the launch and growth of scalable ventures. This program provides equity-based investment rather than traditional grants, strategically designed to provide crucial early-stage capital. This approach ensures a shared commitment between MIF and the founding teams, aligning interests for long-term growth and success. By offering equity, MIF aims to be a genuine partner, not just a funder, participating in the journey and providing the necessary backing to help BITSian ventures achieve scale and market leadership. Funding is structured to support different stages, with startups able to receive up to ₹50 Lakhs when co-investing with another investor, or up to ₹20 Lakhs without a co-investment partner. The available cheque size ranges from a minimum of INR 5 Lakhs to a maximum of INR 50 Lakhs. This capital is intended to catalyze the development and scaling of promising BITSian-led startups. Beyond financial backing, MIF offers extensive non-monetary support, recognizing that early-stage startups require more than just capital. This includes specialized coaching and mentorship provided by seasoned professionals from Silicon Road Ventures, PIEDS' network, and various partner organizations. These mentors bring invaluable industry experience, strategic guidance, and practical advice, helping founders navigate common startup challenges. Founders also gain access to an active community of BITSian builders and entrepreneurs, fostering peer learning, collaboration, and networking opportunities. This community acts as a powerful support system, enabling knowledge exchange and resource sharing. Additionally, select companies may benefit from a strategic bridge to the US market via Silicon Road Ventures, alongside opportunities for institutional VC access, with potential follow-on cheques ranging from $250K to $5M from Silicon Road Ventures. This comprehensive support system is geared towards building robust, sustainable businesses.

opportunities.title: PrISE Cohort 3.0: Incubation Program for Early-Stage Social Enterprises
opportunities.provider: Bala Vikasa Center for Social and Responsible Business
opportunities.description: Incubation program for early-stage social enterprises focused on scalable social and environmental solutions.
opportunities.eligibility: The program specifically targets early-stage social enterprises, prioritizing those actively engaged in idea validation or pilot stages, where structured support can have the most transformative impact. Founders must be actively building or piloting their solution to a significant social or environmental problem, demonstrating a commitment beyond theoretical concepts. Applicants need…The program specifically targets early-stage social enterprises, prioritizing those actively engaged in idea validation or pilot stages, where structured support can have the most transformative impact. Applicants need to clearly articulate the specific problem their venture addresses, proving a deep understanding of the issue and its beneficiaries. Furthermore, ventures must show potential for scalable social or environmental impact, indicating their capacity to achieve widespread positive change. Consistent commitment from participants throughout the entire incubation period is also a crucial requirement to maximize learning and growth.
opportunities.benefits: as incubation. Fund TypeIncubationPersonalized mentorship and structured workshops covering business strategy, financial planning, fundraising readiness, marketing, and product development. Facilitated access to impact investors, CSR partnerships, and external grant opportunities. Extensive networking, market access, and visibility through connections with NGOs, foundations, and industry leaders. Support for go-to-market and growth strategies. Support ProvidedMentorshipTrainingNetworkingMarket AccessIncubation
opportunities.timeline: The selection process is a two-stage evaluation designed to identify enterprises with strong potential for impact and scalability. The initial assessment focuses on the clarity of the problem-solution fit, any early validation or traction achieved, and the overall vision for scalable impact. Shortlisted candidates from this initial application phase will…The selection process is a two-stage evaluation designed to identify enterprises with strong potential for impact and scalability. Shortlisted candidates from this initial application phase will then receive further questions designed to delve deeper into their business model, team capabilities, and market understanding. Finally, the Bala Vikasa CSRB team conducts interviews with selected founders, providing an opportunity for a comprehensive assessment of their readiness and commitment to the program. Selection ProcessA two-stage evaluation: initial assessment of problem-solution fit, early validation, and scalable impact. Shortlisted candidates receive further questions on business model and team. Finally, selected founders undergo interviews with the Bala Vikasa CSRB team.
opportunities.about: Apply NowThe Bala Vikasa Center for Social and Responsible Business (BVCSRB) is an initiative of Bala Vikasa, a highly respected non-profit organization with a long-standing track record in community development and social innovation in India. BVCSRB leverages Bala Vikasa's deep field experience to support social enterprises that offer market-based solutions to pressing social and environmental challenges. Their mission is to empower founders to build financially sustainable ventures that deliver significant, measurable impact. PrISE (Program for Innovation in Social Entrepreneurship) Cohort 3. 0 is an incubation program specifically designed for early-stage social enterprises. It is organized by the Bala Vikasa Center for Social and Responsible Business (BVCSRB), an organization focused on supporting ventures that provide scalable social and environmental solutions. The program aims to foster the growth of impact-driven startups by strengthening their business models and preparing them for successful fundraising and scaling. The PrISE program offers comprehensive non-monetary support, including personalized mentorship from sector experts and seasoned practitioners, tailored to the specific needs of each enterprise. Participants benefit from structured workshops covering crucial aspects such as developing robust business strategies, mastering financial planning and fundraising readiness, effective marketing and branding, and agile product development. While the program itself does not provide direct funding, it actively facilitates access to various funding avenues, including introductions to impact investors, fostering CSR partnerships, and connecting participants with external grant opportunities, preparing them for future capital raises. A key component of the program is its emphasis on ecosystem development, connecting participants with a wide network of NGOs, foundations, and industry leaders. These connections provide valuable market access, visibility, and networking opportunities, crucial for strengthening the enterprises' market presence and achieving their social impact goals. The overarching objective is to help founders clarify their go-to-market and growth strategies, enabling them to effectively balance financial sustainability with their core social mission.

opportunities.title: Bihar Startup Policy 2022: Success Fee Scheme
opportunities.provider: Department of Industries, Government of Bihar
opportunities.description: Success fee for Bihar-registered startups that secured external funding.
opportunities.eligibility: The startup must be an early-stage certified entity under the Bihar Startup Policy. The startup must be registered in Bihar. The startup must have successfully secured funding from angel investors or institutional investors recognized by SEBI or RBI. Proof of a signed funding agreement and actual receipt of funds is… The startup must be an early-stage certified entity under the Bihar Startup Policy. Proof of a signed funding agreement and actual receipt of funds is required. DPIIT RequiredPrior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: as seed funding with no equity dilution. Fund TypeSeed FundingEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableA financial incentive of 2% of external investment secured from approved angel or institutional investors. This defrays fundraising costs including legal, due-diligence, financial advisory, and valuation charges, supporting the startup's financial health during funding milestones. Support ProvidedCashDisbursementMilestone Based
opportunities.timeline: To apply for the Success Fee Scheme, eligible startups must first register on the official Bihar Startup Portal (startup. bihar. gov. in). The application process involves completing an online application form, which requires detailed information about the startup, the investment secured, and the investors involved. Applicants are required to upload all mandatory supporting…ApplicationsRolling BasisTo apply for the Success Fee Scheme, eligible startups must first register on the official Bihar Startup Portal (startup. Applicants are required to upload all mandatory supporting documents, including the signed investment agreement with eligible angel or institutional investors, official proof of funds receipt (such as FIRC or bank statements confirming the transaction), and the startup's valid DPIIT recognition certificate. Additionally, complete bank details for the disbursement of the success fee must be provided. Once submitted, the application will undergo a thorough verification process by the Department of Industries, Government of Bihar, to ensure full compliance with the Bihar Startup Policy 2022 guidelines and the eligibility criteria of the Success Fee Scheme. Upon successful verification and approval, the success fee will be disbursed to the startup's registered bank account. Selection ProcessApplications submitted online are thoroughly verified by the Department of Industries for compliance with policy guidelines and eligibility. Upon successful verification and approval, the fee is disbursed to the startup's account. Required DocumentsSigned Investment AgreementpdfFIRC/Proof of Fund ReceiptpdfDPIIT CertificatepdfBank DetailsanyContact InformationPhone:1800-345-6214Email:[email protected]
opportunities.about: The Bihar Startup Policy 2022, overseen by the Department of Industries, Government of Bihar, introduces the 'Success Fee Scheme' to empower early-stage startups within the state. This comprehensive policy aims to cultivate a thriving entrepreneurial ecosystem, stimulate innovation, and deliver diverse incentives for nascent ventures throughout Bihar, reflecting the government's steadfast commitment to supporting local startups from their foundational stages and positioning the state as a hub for innovation and growth. CallContactApply NowThis distinctive scheme offers a financial incentive equal to 2% of the total investment a startup successfully mobilizes from external sources. To qualify, these investments must originate from angel investors or recognized institutional investors, specifically those approved by SEBI or RBI. The primary objective of this fee is to alleviate the financial burden associated with securing external capital by defraying various costs incurred during the fundraising process. Securing external investment often comes with significant transactional costs that can strain an early-stage startup's finances. This scheme directly addresses this challenge by reimbursing a portion of these essential expenditures, thereby encouraging startups to pursue external capital without undue financial burden. It acts as a financial reward for successful fundraising, recognizing the effort and resources invested in attracting external capital. Eligible expenses for which this success fee provides relief include essential legal fees, necessary due-diligence charges, financial advisory fees, valuation charges, and other transaction-related expenditures inherent in investment rounds. By covering a portion of these significant costs, the scheme directly supports the startup's financial health and operational continuity as it achieves critical funding milestones and scales its operations. This initiative is a core component of the broader Bihar Startup Policy 2022, which collectively endeavors to create a comprehensive and supportive framework for entrepreneurial development across the state. It aims to reduce barriers to capital access and incentivize further investment into the region's startup landscape. The Department of Industries, Government of Bihar, is the nodal agency responsible for industrial development and promoting entrepreneurship in the state. Its mandate includes formulating and implementing policies that attract investment, generate employment, and foster a robust business environment.

opportunities.title: Bihar Startup Policy 2022: Zero Patent Filing Cost Scheme
opportunities.provider: Department of Industries, Government of Bihar
opportunities.description: 100% reimbursement of patent filing costs for Bihar-registered certified startups.
opportunities.eligibility: The startup must be officially certified under the Bihar Startup Policy. This certification process ensures that the startup meets the state's criteria for innovation, potential for growth, and contribution to the local economy, thereby focusing support on genuine early-stage ventures. The startup must be legally registered within the geographical boundaries… The startup must be officially certified under the Bihar Startup Policy. The startup must be legally registered within the geographical boundaries of the state of Bihar. This ensures that the benefits of the scheme are directed towards businesses that are actively contributing to Bihar's economic development and employment. The patent application for which reimbursement is sought must be filed directly in the startup's name. This criterion ensures that the intellectual property being protected is directly owned by the applicant startup, aligning the scheme's benefits with the startup's core innovation and avoiding claims for patents held by individuals or other entities.
opportunities.benefits: as grant with no equity dilution. opportunities.timeline: Applications for reimbursement are to be submitted exclusively through the official Department of Industries / Startup Bihar online portal. This digital platform streamlines the submission process, making it accessible to startups across the state. Applicants are required to provide a specific set of documents to substantiate their claim. These typically…ApplicationsRolling Basis Applications for reimbursement are to be submitted exclusively through the official Department of Industries / Startup Bihar online portal. These typically include comprehensive patent filing receipts issued by the respective patent offices, detailed invoices from patent attorneys outlining their services and fees, and accurate bank details for the direct transfer of the reimbursement amount. Additional supporting documents, such as a grant certificate (if the patent has already been granted) can also be submitted to strengthen the application. The reimbursement process is initiated and completed only after the patent application has been officially filed or the patent has been granted, whichever is applicable based on the policy norms. The submitted documents undergo a thorough verification process by the Department of Industries to ensure compliance with all conditions of the Bihar Startup Policy 2022 before the financial support is disbursed. This structured approach ensures accountability and proper utilization of public funds. opportunities.about: The Bihar Startup Policy 2022 offers a Zero Patent Filing Cost Scheme, a pivotal initiative from the Department of Industries, Government of Bihar, under its broader 'Startup Bihar' program. The Department of Industries plays a crucial role in fostering industrial growth and innovation across the state. Through 'Startup Bihar,' it is specifically designed to cultivate a thriving entrepreneurial ecosystem by providing comprehensive support to early-stage ventures. This particular scheme directly addresses a significant barrier for innovative startups: the high costs associated with intellectual property protection. By alleviating this financial burden, the policy aims to actively encourage and accelerate the pace of innovation among local startups, ensuring their valuable creations are legally protected. The scheme provides 100% reimbursement for the costs incurred in filing domestic patent applications, making it easier for Bihar-based innovators to secure their intellectual assets within India. Furthermore, recognizing the global potential of local innovations, the policy also offers full cost reimbursement for international patent filings. This includes applications pursued under the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) or directly in foreign jurisdictions, adhering strictly to the specified policy norms which may include caps on certain expenditures or requirements for patent validity. This comprehensive coverage is designed to be all-encompassing, including various essential expenditures such as attorney fees for legal drafting and consultation, official filing fees charged by patent offices, examination fees for technical review, and any other statutory charges associated with the intricate patent application process, both nationally and internationally. The financial support provided through this scheme is specifically intended to cover the direct, verifiable costs of patent acquisition. It empowers startups to protect their foundational intellectual assets without significant upfront financial strain, which can be a critical factor for nascent businesses. The reimbursement mechanism is structured to be transparent and efficient, ensuring that qualifying startups can reclaim their legitimate expenses after the patent application has been filed and verified against policy guidelines. This proactive support reinforces Bihar's commitment to nurturing a competitive and innovative startup landscape.

opportunities.title: 100X.VC Investment Program
opportunities.description: Seed investment for early-stage Indian startups through iSAFE notes, includes mentorship.
opportunities.eligibility: The program is designed for founders actively building their startup from India. This ensures that the founders are deeply embedded in the Indian startup ecosystem and can leverage local market insights. We look for passionate founders who are dedicated full-time to their venture, demonstrating a strong commitment to scaling. Startups… The program is designed for founders actively building their startup from India. Startups should be looking to raise their first institutional cheque. This focus allows 100X. VC to be a foundational investor, providing crucial early capital that helps validate the business model and attract subsequent larger funding rounds. It targets companies that are beyond the idea stage but have not yet secured significant venture capital. The applicant must be an incorporated Indian entity for legal and due diligence purposes. This requirement is critical for legal compliance, facilitating the investment process, and ensuring the startup operates within the regulatory framework of India. This typically includes Private Limited Companies (Pvt Ltd) or Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs).
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹1. 25 Cr as seed funding. AmountUpto ₹1. 25 CrFund TypeSeed FundingRepaymentNon-RepayableSelected startups receive a fixed investment of ₹1. 25 crore via an iSAFE note (15% future equity). Benefits include a 6-week in-person HomeRun Masterclass Series in Mumbai, insights from industry experts, experienced mentors, and successful founders. Participants gain access to 100X. VC's extensive business network and a VC Pitch Day to secure follow-on funding. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipNetworkingTrainingIncubationDisbursementLump SumProgram Duration1 monthsBootstrap FriendlyYes
opportunities.timeline: Submit the online application: The initial step involves completing a comprehensive online application form, which requires detailed information about your startup, team, business model, market opportunity, and traction. This serves as the first impression of your venture for the 100X. VC team. Founders are contacted within 7-10 working days if selected…ApplicationsRolling Basis Submit the online application: The initial step involves completing a comprehensive online application form, which requires detailed information about your startup, team, business model, market opportunity, and traction. Founders are contacted within 7-10 working days if selected for further evaluation: Following the initial review of applications, promising startups are contacted by the 100X. VC team within this timeframe for an initial screening call. This call helps assess alignment with 100X. VC's investment thesis and provides an opportunity for founders to elaborate on their vision and progress. Shortlisted startups are invited to Mumbai for an interview: Candidates who pass the screening call are then invited to an in-person interview round in Mumbai. This crucial stage allows the 100X. VC partners to meet the founding team, understand their dynamics, delve deeper into their business model, and evaluate their potential for growth and market disruption. A successful interview results in a term sheet offer: If the interview process is successful and the team demonstrates strong potential, a non-binding term sheet outlining the investment terms, including the iSAFE note structure, is extended. This signifies 100X. VC's commitment to invest in your startup. Funding is disbursed after legal compliance, due diligence, and iSAFE note signing: Upon acceptance of the term sheet, 100X. VC initiates a thorough legal compliance and due diligence process to verify all company details and legal standings. Once this is successfully completed, and the India SAFE Notes are officially signed, the investment of ₹1. 25 crore is promptly disbursed to the startup's bank account, marking the official entry into the 100X. VC portfolio. Selection ProcessSubmit online application. Promising startups receive an initial screening call. Shortlisted candidates are invited for an in-person interview in Mumbai. Successful interviews lead to a term sheet offer. Funding is disbursed after legal compliance, due diligence, and iSAFE note signing. Contact InformationEmail:[email protected]
opportunities.about: 100X. VC is a pioneering Venture Capital firm in India, renowned for being the first to invest in early-stage startups using founder-friendly India SAFE Notes. The firm's core mission is to champion innovation across India by identifying and empowering nascent businesses. It aims to fund and nurture seed-stage startups, significantly contributing to the growth and dynamism of the Indian startup ecosystem. VC actively seeks out promising ventures and audacious founders from across the country to support their initial growth phases with strategic capital and extensive guidance. The program offers a fixed investment of ₹1. 25 crore to each selected startup. This funding is provided through an iSAFE note, which translates to 15% of future equity ownership in the company. India SAFE Notes (Simple Agreement for Future Equity) are specifically designed to be founder-friendly, offering a streamlined investment mechanism that defers complex valuation discussions to a later equity funding round. This structure provides quick capital infusion without immediate valuation complexities, allowing founders to focus entirely on product development and market penetration. Following successful legal compliance and due diligence, and the signing of the iSAFE notes, the investment amount is immediately disbursed directly into the startup's bank account. Beyond capital: 100X. VC provides substantial non-monetary support crucial for early-stage success. This includes the exclusive 6-week in-person HomeRun Masterclass Series in Mumbai, an intensive program featuring a structured curriculum designed to enhance business building skills across various domains. Participants gain insights from a diverse array of industry experts, experienced mentors, and successful founders, covering critical aspects like product-market fit, fundraising strategies, and leadership development. Startups also gain access to 100X. VC's extensive business network, comprising seasoned advisors, dedicated mentors, large corporates, and a vibrant community of alumni founders. This network provides unparalleled opportunities for connections, collaborations, and strategic partnerships. The program culminates in a VC Pitch Day, a high-stakes event offering portfolio companies an opportunity to present their progress and vision to a carefully curated selection of top-tier investors for their next funding round, thereby accelerating their access to follow-on capital.

opportunities.title: Market Acceleration Grant
opportunities.description: Supports early-revenue tech startups in Kerala with up to ₹10L for market acceleration.
opportunities.eligibility: Applicants must be in the early revenue stage. This focus ensures that the grant supports ventures that have already validated their product or service and are now ready to scale their market reach. Applicants must own an innovative technology-based product. The grant specifically targets technological innovation as a driver for… Applicants must be in the early revenue stage. The grant specifically targets technological innovation as a driver for economic growth and competitiveness within Kerala's startup ecosystem. Applicants must hold a valid KSUM Unique ID. This requirement ensures that applicants are formally recognized within the KSUM ecosystem, facilitating streamlined processing and access to other KSUM resources. Applicants must be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, LLP, or OPC with active MCA status. This ensures legal compliance and a structured business entity, vital for managing grants and scaling operations. Startups that have previously availed an Idea Grant or Productization Grant are eligible only for the remaining eligible amount. This policy prevents double-dipping while still allowing continuous support for promising ventures through different growth stages, up to the overall grant ceiling. Prior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹10 Lakhs as grant with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹10 LakhsFund TypeGrantEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableReceive up to ₹10 lakhs financial aid, disbursed milestone-based, to strengthen market presence. Funds can be invested in marketing, customer acquisition, branding, and product improvements for market acceleration. Startups contribute 30% margin money. Support ProvidedCashDisbursementMilestone Based
opportunities.timeline: Applications are initially screened by KSUM officials, with shortlisted startups notified via email. This initial screening assesses adherence to basic eligibility criteria and the completeness of the application. An Expert Committee, comprising industry veterans, investors, and domain specialists, evaluates pitches based on a comprehensive set of criteria including the strength…ApplicationsRolling BasisApplications are initially screened by KSUM officials, with shortlisted startups notified via email. An Expert Committee, comprising industry veterans, investors, and domain specialists, evaluates pitches based on a comprehensive set of criteria including the strength of the team, the uniqueness and market potential of the product innovation, the proposed marketing and customer acquisition strategies, financial projections, and the clarity of the grant utilization plan. The committee's thorough review ensures that only the most promising and impactful startups receive funding. Approved recommendations lead to formal document submission and agreement execution with KSUM, solidifying the partnership. Milestone-based fund disbursement follows, requiring detailed progress reports and verification of achieved targets for subsequent tranches. This structured approach ensures accountability and aligns the grant disbursement with the startup's actual market acceleration progress. Selection ProcessApplications are initially screened by KSUM officials. Shortlisted startups pitch to an Expert Committee that evaluates team, product potential, marketing strategy, financial projections, and grant utilization. Approved recommendations lead to agreement execution. Required DocumentsCA-certified No Dues & Margin Money Certificate with UDINanyRevenue/Investment ProofanyIncorporation DocumentsanyPAN CardanyKSUM Unique IDanyBank DetailsanyBalance Sheet for last two yearsanyStamp PaperanyContact InformationContact:Shahil, HaneefPhone:08047180470Email:[email protected]
opportunities.about: The Market Acceleration Grant, a key initiative of the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM), is specifically designed to empower startups within Kerala that have achieved the early revenue stage. KSUM, a government agency, plays a pivotal role in nurturing the state's burgeoning startup ecosystem, offering various programs to support innovation and growth. Its mandate includes fostering an enabling environment for technological entrepreneurship, providing crucial resources, and facilitating market entry and expansion for promising ventures. This particular scheme focuses on providing strategic financial assistance to accelerate market expansion and bolster the commercial viability of these nascent enterprises, recognizing that successful market penetration is critical for long-term sustainability. Successful applicants can receive financial aid up to ₹10 lakhs, aimed at strengthening their market presence. The grant operates on a milestone-based disbursement model, where funds are released in tranches upon achieving pre-defined targets, aligning with KSUM's standard operational guidelines. This ensures accountability and progress-driven utilization of funds. Importantly, startups that have previously benefited from KSUM's Idea Grant or Productization Grant are still eligible, provided they apply for the remaining balance up to the maximum grant ceiling, demonstrating KSUM's commitment to continuous support for its nurtured startups. The funding is versatile, allowing startups to invest in critical areas such as enhancing marketing efforts, optimizing customer acquisition strategies, refining branding, and implementing necessary product improvements. These allocations are intended to directly contribute to revenue generation activities, directly impacting the startup's growth trajectory. The grant understands that market access and effective customer outreach are often capital-intensive, and this support aims to bridge that gap. Furthermore, to foster commitment and shared responsibility, the scheme mandates that startups contribute 30% of the approved grant amount as margin money, ensuring a co-investment approach to their growth trajectory. This ensures that startups have a vested interest in the successful execution of their market acceleration plans.

opportunities.title: Next Bharat Residency Program
opportunities.provider: Next Bharat Ventures IFSC Private Limited
opportunities.description: Equity and mentorship for revenue-generating impact ventures in India's rural and informal economies.
opportunities.eligibility: Impact-oriented, for-profit enterprises focused on increasing incomes for India's informal urban (India 2) or rural (India 3) economies are eligible. Applicants must be revenue-generating at the time of application, demonstrating early market traction and a scalable business model. Ventures must be actively planning to raise equity funding within the next… Impact-oriented, for-profit enterprises focused on increasing incomes for India's informal urban (India 2) or rural (India 3) economies are eligible. Ventures must be actively planning to raise equity funding within the next 3-6 months; the program is structured to prepare founders for this milestone. Full attendance for the entire program duration is mandatory; completion of the program is a prerequisite for funding eligibility, though it does not guarantee investment. The highest equity holder of the company is required to attend all in-person sessions in Bengaluru; co-founders may attend on a need-basis but cannot substitute for the primary founder. Eligible ventures must demonstrate measurable social impact alongside commercial viability, with operations directly benefiting low-income or rural communities. Startups from sectors including agriculture, food processing, rural livelihoods, artisan crafts, waste management, sustainability, and allied impact sectors are encouraged to apply.
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹5 Cr as accelerator. AmountUpto ₹5 CrFund TypeAcceleratorEquity funding up to INR 5 crore, plus a nominal living stipend. Participants get experienced mentorship, access to Suzuki group networks, market and partnership opportunities. Join a pan-India community for peer collaboration and long-term support. Hybrid 3-month program with intensive in-person sessions.
opportunities.timeline: Step 1: Application Review : the Next Bharat team evaluates all applications and shortlists potential candidates based on impact focus, revenue traction, and scalability. Step 2: Interviews : shortlisted founders participate in 2-3 rounds of interviews to assess fit, commitment, and growth potential. Step 3: Field Visit : investment representatives… Step 1: Application Review : the Next Bharat team evaluates all applications and shortlists potential candidates based on impact focus, revenue traction, and scalability. Step 3: Field Visit : investment representatives conduct an on-site visit to the startup's operations before final selection. Step 4: Final Selection : successful applicants are notified and onboarding begins for the upcoming cohort. Selection ProcessApplication review by Next Bharat team, shortlisting based on impact, revenue, and scalability. Followed by 2-3 rounds of interviews. Investment representatives then conduct an on-site field visit before final selection. Contact InformationEmail:[email protected]
opportunities.about: The Next Bharat Residency Program is spearheaded by Next Bharat Ventures IFSC Private Limited, a Suzuki Initiative committed to empowering impact entrepreneurs across India. The program is built around a clear premise: India's next wave of economic growth will be powered by its informal urban (India 2) and rural (India 3) economies, home to over one billion people with household incomes ranging from Rs. 1. 5 lakh to Rs. 10 lakh per year. These communities represent a vast, underserved market with enormous potential. The program runs in a hybrid format over 3 months, combining three weeks of intensive in-person sessions in Bengaluru with ongoing virtual engagement. Selected founders receive equity-based funding of up to INR 5 crore to scale their venture, and a nominal stipend to cover living expenses during the in-person residency. Beyond capital, participants gain access to experienced mentors who understand the ground realities of building impactful businesses, including operational, market, and regulatory challenges specific to rural and informal sectors. A distinguishing feature of the program is its connection to the Suzuki group's networks across India and Japan, opening up meaningful market access and partnership opportunities for portfolio companies. Participants also join a growing pan-India community of fellow impact entrepreneurs, enabling peer collaboration, shared learning, and long-term support well beyond the program duration. Past cohort companies have operated in sustainable agriculture, food processing, artisan crafts, dairy, waste management, and rural livelihoods.

opportunities.provider: Agri-Food Business Incubation Centre (AFBIC), IIT Kharagpur
opportunities.description: Grant up to ₹25L for West Bengal-based agri-startups at MVP/expansion stage.
opportunities.eligibility: Either the founder must be from West Bengal, or the startup must be originating from West Bengal Startup must be at MVP, beta, or expansion stage of its product or service Working in agriculture, food processing, or allied sectors Typically a registered company (private limited or LLP) Either the founder must be from West Bengal, or the startup must be originating from West Bengal Startup must be at MVP, beta, or expansion stage of its product or service Working in agriculture, food processing, or allied sectors Typically a registered company (private limited or LLP)
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹25 Lakhs as incubation with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹25 LakhsFund TypeIncubationEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableUp to ₹25 lakh in non-dilutive grant-in-aid, disbursed by AFBIC after selection and milestone-based performance evaluation. Selected startups also get one month of online mentorship and a 15-day on-campus incubation workshop at IIT Kharagpur, plus access to the institute's domain experts, lab infrastructure, and the wider AFBIC agri-startup network. opportunities.timeline: Submit the AFBIC Cohort 3. 0 application form before 30 May 2026 Initial screening by AFBIC for eligibility and AIP-track fit Shortlisted startups go through training and performance evaluation Final recommendation by the AFBIC committee for grant-in-aid disbursementApplication FeeFree Submit the AFBIC Cohort 3. 0 application form before 30 May 2026 Initial screening by AFBIC for eligibility and AIP-track fit Shortlisted startups go through training and performance evaluation Final recommendation by the AFBIC committee for grant-in-aid disbursement Contact InformationPhone:8367808088, 7838697692Email:[email protected]
opportunities.about: The Agri-Startup Incubation Programme (AIP) is run by the Agri-Food Business Incubation Centre (AFBIC) at IIT Kharagpur under the RKVY-RAFTAAR program of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Govt. of India. It backs agri-food startups that have moved past the idea stage and are at MVP, beta, or expansion stage of their product or service. Selected startups receive grant-in-aid of up to ₹25 lakh, disbursed after the selection, training, and performance evaluation process. The programme also provides one month of online mentorship and a 15-day on-campus incubation workshop at IIT Kharagpur, giving founders structured exposure to the institute's Agricultural and Food Engineering department, domain experts, and AFBIC's agri-startup network. AIP is designed for West Bengal–linked ventures working in agriculture, food processing, and allied sectors. The grant is non-dilutive: AFBIC does not take equity. Cohort 3. 0 applications close on 30 May 2026.

opportunities.title: Access Programme
opportunities.description: Abu Dhabi-based program for pre-seed to Series A tech startups offering cash, in-kind incentives, and market access.
opportunities.eligibility: The program is specifically designed for pre-seed to Series A startups, meaning it targets companies from their initial development phase up to those that have achieved significant traction and are seeking to raise their first institutional rounds of funding. Applicants should be established startups that have a demonstrable product or…The program is specifically designed for pre-seed to Series A startups, meaning it targets companies from their initial development phase up to those that have achieved significant traction and are seeking to raise their first institutional rounds of funding. Applicants should be established startups that have a demonstrable product or service, are seeking substantial investment opportunities, and are looking to strategically gain a strong foothold in Abu Dhabi to scale their business within the MENA region and beyond. A crucial requirement for participation is that at least one founder must commit to long-term relocation and building a core team out of Abu Dhabi. This ensures active engagement with the Hub71 ecosystem, full utilization of local resources, and contribution to the regional tech talent pool. To apply, startups must submit a comprehensive pitch deck in PDF format. This deck should clearly highlight the problem they are solving, their proposed solution, unique value proposition, sustainable business model, competitive landscape, target market, existing traction or milestones achieved, funds raised to date, profiles of the founders and founding team, and detailed plans for leveraging Hub71 and the Abu Dhabi ecosystem for their growth. Prior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: Offers ₹2. 5 Lakhs – ₹5 Lakhs as accelerator. Amount₹2. 5 Lakhs – ₹5 LakhsFund TypeAcceleratorRepaymentNon-RepayableSelected startups receive AED 250,000 in cash in exchange for equity via a SAFE note, alongside AED 250,000 in flexible in-kind incentives. High-performing startups demonstrating strong commitment are eligible for an additional AED 250,000 for further equity, bringing the total potential package to AED 750,000. In-kind benefits include premium office space, essential health insurance, and housing assistance. Additionally, the program provides strategic connections to capital providers, corporate partners, and experienced mentors to accelerate growth. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipCo WorkingLegalNetworkingMarket AccessTrainingIncubationDisbursementTranchedProgram Duration12 months
opportunities.timeline: The application process is designed to identify high-potential startups ready to scale. Initially, applicants must submit a complete application form providing a concise yet thorough overview of their startup, including the problem they address, their innovative solution, product details, and their business model. This initial submission is critical for demonstrating…Avg. Processing90 daysThe application process is designed to identify high-potential startups ready to scale. This initial submission is critical for demonstrating foundational understanding and market potential. If successful in the initial screening, applicants will proceed to deliver a series of pitch presentations. These presentations are conducted to the Hub71 team, a panel of experienced investors, and key corporate and government partners, allowing for comprehensive evaluation from various perspectives. A final selection committee, comprising industry experts and Hub71 leadership, makes the ultimate decision after this rigorous four-stage evaluation process, which typically includes initial online application review, preliminary interviews, advanced pitching rounds, and due diligence checks. This multi-stage process ensures that only the most promising startups, aligned with Hub71's vision, are accepted into the program. Required DocumentsPitch Deckpdf
opportunities.about: Hub71 Access Programme supports pre-seed to Series A tech startups looking to establish and scale their businesses in Abu Dhabi. Hub71 is a global tech ecosystem, established by the Abu Dhabi government, that actively fosters innovation and enables founders to expand into the dynamic UAE capital's marketplace. It strategically connects startups with a community of capital providers, corporate partners, and experienced mentors, aiming to accelerate growth and strengthen the tech landscape in the region. Selected startups receive AED 250,000 in flexible in-kind incentives, which can be uniquely customized and utilized with various approved service providers within the Hub71 network. Additionally, they receive AED 250,000 in cash in exchange for equity via a SAFE note, providing essential capital for early-stage development and operations. High-performing startups, demonstrating strong commitment to building and expanding in Abu Dhabi, are eligible for an additional top-up of AED 250,000 in exchange for further equity by the end of the one-year program. This structured approach brings the potential total monetary and in-kind incentives to a significant AED 750,000, designed to provide substantial runway for growth. The comprehensive in-kind incentives cover a wide range of critical support services, ensuring startups have the resources they need to thrive. These include premium office space within Hub71's state-of-the-art facilities, essential health insurance, housing assistance, dedicated workforce support, streamlined licensing and visa assistance to facilitate relocation, marketing and branding support, critical legal and financial advisory services, and efficient back-office support. The program also offers specialized upskilling opportunities and targeted growth marketing support to help startups acquire and retain customers effectively. The cash component, provided for equity, is flexible and intended to support general operational expenses, product development, and overall growth strategies within the startup. Beyond financial incentives, the program offers an intensive 12-month incubation experience. This includes a focused 3-month guided track powered by Techstars, renowned for its accelerator methodology, providing unparalleled access to their global network of mentors for tailored strategic advice and operational guidance. Startups gain invaluable introductions to key partners within Hub71's extensive network, fostering potential collaboration opportunities with leading corporations, government entities, and investors. The program culminates in a fundraising showcase with venture capitalists and a public Demo Day, maximizing visibility and investment prospects for participating startups.

Not affiliated with any government body.Access Programme | Startup Grants IndiaAccess ProgrammeFunding amountClosing dateEligibilityBenefitsTimelinesFAQsHub71Access ProgrammeFunding amountClosing dateAbu Dhabi-based program for pre-seed to Series A tech startups offering cash, in-kind incentives, and market access.StageMVP, Early RevenueTypeAcceleratorLocationInternationalApply NowApply NowApply NowAbout this grantHub71 Access Programme supports pre-seed to Series A tech startups looking to establish and scale their businesses in Abu Dhabi. Hub71 is a global tech ecosystem, established by the Abu Dhabi government, that actively fosters innovation and enables founders to expand into the dynamic UAE capital's marketplace. It strategically connects startups with a community of capital providers, corporate partners, and experienced mentors, aiming to accelerate growth and strengthen the tech landscape in the region. Selected startups receive AED 250,000 in flexible in-kind incentives, which can be uniquely customized and utilized with various approved service providers within the Hub71 network. Additionally, they receive AED 250,000 in cash in exchange for equity via a SAFE note, providing essential capital for early-stage development and operations. High-performing startups, demonstrating strong commitment to building and expanding in Abu Dhabi, are eligible for an additional top-up of AED 250,000 in exchange for further equity by the end of the one-year program. This structured approach brings the potential total monetary and in-kind incentives to a significant AED 750,000, designed to provide substantial runway for growth. The comprehensive in-kind incentives cover a wide range of critical support services, ensuring startups have the resources they need to thrive. These include premium office space within Hub71's state-of-the-art facilities, essential health insurance, housing assistance, dedicated workforce support, streamlined licensing and visa assistance to facilitate relocation, marketing and branding support, critical legal and financial advisory services, and efficient back-office support. The program also offers specialized upskilling opportunities and targeted growth marketing support to help startups acquire and retain customers effectively. The cash component, provided for equity, is flexible and intended to support general operational expenses, product development, and overall growth strategies within the startup. Beyond financial incentives, the program offers an intensive 12-month incubation experience. This includes a focused 3-month guided track powered by Techstars, renowned for its accelerator methodology, providing unparalleled access to their global network of mentors for tailored strategic advice and operational guidance. Startups gain invaluable introductions to key partners within Hub71's extensive network, fostering potential collaboration opportunities with leading corporations, government entities, and investors. The program culminates in a fundraising showcase with venture capitalists and a public Demo Day, maximizing visibility and investment prospects for participating startups.ProviderHub71Mubadala Investment CompanyHub71 is Abu Dhabi's flagship technology ecosystem, launched in 2019 as an initiative of the Abu Dhabi government in partnership with Mubadala Investment Company, ADQ, and other anchor partners.…Government • Est. 2019 • Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab EmiratesWebsiteLinkedInKnow more →AI OverviewA quick AI-written summary of this grant — amount, deadline, current stage, and why it might fit you.Coming soonOur RecommendationA personalised verdict on this grant for your startup — fit across stage and sector, win probability, competition level, seat availability, and whether it's a hidden gem worth applying to right now.Coming soonEligibilityThe program is specifically designed for pre-seed to Series A startups, meaning it targets companies from their initial development phase up to those that have achieved significant traction and are seeking to raise their first institutional rounds of funding. Applicants should be established startups that have a demonstrable product or…The program is specifically designed for pre-seed to Series A startups, meaning it targets companies from their initial development phase up to those that have achieved significant traction and are seeking to raise their first institutional rounds of funding. Applicants should be established startups that have a demonstrable product or service, are seeking substantial investment opportunities, and are looking to strategically gain a strong foothold in Abu Dhabi to scale their business within the MENA region and beyond. A crucial requirement for participation is that at least one founder must commit to long-term relocation and building a core team out of Abu Dhabi. This ensures active engagement with the Hub71 ecosystem, full utilization of local resources, and contribution to the regional tech talent pool. To apply, startups must submit a comprehensive pitch deck in PDF format. This deck should clearly highlight the problem they are solving, their proposed solution, unique value proposition, sustainable business model, competitive landscape, target market, existing traction or milestones achieved, funds raised to date, profiles of the founders and founding team, and detailed plans for leveraging Hub71 and the Abu Dhabi ecosystem for their growth.Prior Funding AllowedSign in or create a free account to read everything.Sign up to unlock eligibilityBenefits & FundingOffers ₹2.5 Lakhs – ₹5 Lakhs as accelerator.Amount₹2.5 Lakhs – ₹5 LakhsFund TypeAcceleratorRepaymentNon-RepayableSelected startups receive AED 250,000 in cash in exchange for equity via a SAFE note, alongside AED 250,000 in flexible in-kind incentives. High-performing startups demonstrating strong commitment are eligible for an additional AED 250,000 for further equity, bringing the total potential package to AED 750,000. In-kind benefits include premium office space, essential health insurance, and housing assistance. Additionally, the program provides strategic connections to capital providers, corporate partners, and experienced mentors to accelerate growth.Support ProvidedCashMentorshipCo WorkingLegalNetworkingMarket AccessTrainingIncub

opportunities.title: Assistance for Participation in Acceleration Programs
opportunities.provider: Department of Industries & Commerce, Government of Haryana
opportunities.description: Reimbursement for Haryana DPIIT-recognized startups participating in acceleration programs.
opportunities.eligibility: The startup must possess valid recognition from the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Government of India. This recognition is crucial as it validates the startup's innovative nature and allows access to various government support schemes. The startup must be officially registered and based within the state… The startup must possess valid recognition from the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Government of India. The startup must be officially registered and based within the state of Haryana. This criterion ensures that the program directly benefits the state's economy and its local entrepreneurial talent pool. The startup must be actively participating or have received an acceptance into a bona fide domestic or global accelerator program. A bona fide program is typically one with a structured curriculum, mentorship, and a proven track record of helping startups grow. A formal acceptance letter or offer of participation from the accelerator program must be submitted as proof of enrollment. Comprehensive bills and invoices for program fees and all related expenses, such as travel and lodging, are required to process the reimbursement claim. DPIIT RequiredPrior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹2. 5 Lakhs as seed funding with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹2. 5 LakhsFund TypeSeed FundingEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableReimbursement up to ₹2,50,000 annually for accelerator program fees, travel, lodging, and operational costs. This support facilitates access to structured growth methodologies, expert mentorship, networking with investors and industry leaders, and exposure to broader markets via participation in recognized domestic or international acceleration programs. Support ProvidedCashDisbursementReimbursement
opportunities.timeline: Applications must be submitted exclusively through the official Invest Haryana portal, which serves as the central platform for all startup-related policies and incentives in the state. Applicants will need to create an account or log in to access the application form. The process typically involves filling out detailed information about…ApplicationsRolling BasisApplications must be submitted exclusively through the official Invest Haryana portal, which serves as the central platform for all startup-related policies and incentives in the state. The process typically involves filling out detailed information about the startup and the accelerator program, followed by the upload of all necessary supporting documents. Required documents include the DPIIT recognition certificate, the official offer or acceptance letter from the accelerator program, comprehensive expense receipts and invoices for all costs for which reimbursement is sought, and the startup's bank details for direct fund transfer. Once submitted, applications undergo a verification process by the Department of Industries & Commerce. Reimbursement is processed upon successful verification and after the startup's participation in the accelerator program has been completed, ensuring that funds are disbursed for actual, incurred expenses. Required DocumentsDPIIT Recognition CertificateanyAccelerator Offer/Acceptance LetteranyProgram Fee and Related Expense ReceiptsanyBank Detailsany
opportunities.about: This program, a pivotal component of the Haryana Startup Policy 2022, offers targeted financial assistance to eligible startups established within the state of Haryana. It is a key initiative administered by the Department of Industries & Commerce, Government of Haryana, through its dedicated platform, Startup Haryana. The department's overarching mission is to cultivate a dynamic and supportive entrepreneurial ecosystem across Haryana, fostering innovation and providing a comprehensive suite of fiscal incentives and enabling infrastructure for emerging businesses to thrive. The core benefit involves reimbursement for specific expenses incurred by startups successfully participating in recognized domestic or international acceleration programs. This financial provision allows eligible startups to claim up to ₹2,50,000 annually. The support is designed to alleviate the financial strain associated with engaging in intensive accelerator experiences, covering program fees, essential travel expenses, lodging, and various other directly associated operational costs. Participation in accelerators offers startups unparalleled access to structured growth methodologies, expert mentorship, valuable networking opportunities with investors and industry leaders, and exposure to broader markets. This program helps ensure that financial limitations do not prevent promising startups from accessing these critical resources. The allocated funds are exclusively for mitigating the financial barriers to accessing premier acceleration opportunities. This ensures that promising Haryana-based startups can benefit from structured growth methodologies, mentorship, and networking without prohibitive financial constraints. Eligible expenses include not only direct program charges but also critical logistical outlays such as transportation and accommodation necessary for active participation throughout the accelerator journey. By supporting participation in these programs, the Haryana government aims to enhance the competitiveness and scalability of its local startup ventures, integrating them into national and global innovation networks.

opportunities.title: WE Engage - Wadhwani Liftoff: PROPEL
opportunities.description: 12-week program for women-led startups in early growth/scaling stages, focusing on market, funding, network.
opportunities.eligibility: The program is exclusively for women-led startups. Women entrepreneurs must hold a minimum of 51% ownership in the company. If previously funded, women entrepreneurs must still hold a minimum of 33% ownership in the venture. Alternatively, women entrepreneurs can qualify if they hold an equal or majority ownership stake, or…The program is exclusively for women-led startups. Alternatively, women entrepreneurs can qualify if they hold an equal or majority ownership stake, or serve as one of the CXOs in the venture. These ownership criteria ensure the program genuinely supports women leaders. Applicants must be from Urban India, and businesses must be legally registered in India. Startups should be in the early growth or scaling stage, actively generating revenue. Applicants must be ready to raise funding, demonstrating a clear business plan and initial traction. The program welcomes startups from all industries. Women-Led OnlyPrior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: as accelerator. Fund TypeAcceleratorTwelve weeks of curriculum co-designed with the Wadhwani Foundation, covering product-market fit, scalable business models, and fundraising readiness. Participants receive strategic guidance from experts who have built and scaled businesses, supply chain support, market-access opportunities across India, and investor connects. WE Hub also facilitates access to grants, seed funding, and debt through its partner network — though no fixed cheque is guaranteed by the programme itself. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipNetworkingMarket AccessIncubationProgram Duration3 months
opportunities.timeline: The application process for the WE Engage – Wadhwani Liftoff: PROPEL program begins with an online application submission, typically through a Google Form, requiring detailed information about the business, team, traction, and funding readiness. Following this, applications undergo a thorough initial screening by the WE Hub and Wadhwani Foundation teams…The application process for the WE Engage – Wadhwani Liftoff: PROPEL program begins with an online application submission, typically through a Google Form, requiring detailed information about the business, team, traction, and funding readiness. Following this, applications undergo a thorough initial screening by the WE Hub and Wadhwani Foundation teams to assess eligibility and strategic alignment. Shortlisted candidates are then invited for subsequent evaluation rounds, which may include virtual interviews or detailed pitch presentations to a panel of industry experts and investors. These stages allow for a deeper understanding of the startup's vision, potential, and the founder's readiness to leverage the program's resources. Final selections are made based on a comprehensive assessment, leading to an invitation to join the competitive 12-week program cohort. Selection ProcessApplications are submitted via the WE Hub Google Form. WE Hub and Wadhwani Foundation review submissions and shortlist women-led startups in the early growth or scaling stage based on revenue traction, scalability of the business model, and readiness to raise funding. Selected cohort participants enter the 12-week structured programme. Contact InformationEmail:[email protected]
opportunities.about: WE Engage – Wadhwani Liftoff: PROPEL is a 12-week, high-impact program specifically designed to empower women entrepreneurs currently navigating the early growth and scaling stages of their businesses. This program is a collaborative effort between WE Hub, an initiative of the Government of Telangana known as India's first state-led incubator dedicated to women entrepreneurs, and the Wadhwani Foundation, a global non-profit focused on accelerating economic development through entrepreneurship. WE Hub has a proven track record of supporting women-led ventures, notably facilitating a significant recoverable grant for Huwel Lifesciences through its partnerships, underscoring its commitment to fostering entrepreneurial success. The core objective of the PROPEL program is to provide participants with strategic access to crucial market opportunities, diverse funding sources, and an expansive professional network. While the program directly offers support, it actively facilitates access to various financial instruments, including grants, seed funding, and debt financing, leveraging WE Hub's extensive partnership ecosystem. This multifaceted approach ensures that entrepreneurs are well-positioned to secure the capital required for significant scaling and expansion. Participants in PROPEL embark on a customized journey that features co-designed modules, personalized mentorship from industry experts, and hands-on bootcamps. These integrated elements are carefully crafted to help entrepreneurs refine their product-market fit, develop effective and scalable business models, and ultimately strengthen their ability to attract and secure external funding. The program's comprehensive focus on market traction, sustainability, and strategic guidance is geared towards propelling women-led businesses to their next level of success. The collaboration aims to amplify the impact on women-led startups across India, contributing to economic growth and job creation.

opportunities.title: EngSUI Seed Fund
opportunities.provider: SIIC IIT Kanpur, Engineers India Limited
opportunities.description: Funding for DPIIT-recognized Indian startups to scale technology-based innovations.
opportunities.eligibility: The applicant must be an entity officially recognized as a 'Startup' by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India. This recognition ensures that the startup adheres to specific criteria, including being incorporated in India for less than… The applicant must be an entity officially recognized as a 'Startup' by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India. This recognition ensures that the startup adheres to specific criteria, including being incorporated in India for less than 10 years, having an annual turnover not exceeding ₹100 crore for any financial year since its incorporation, and working towards innovation, development or improvement of products or processes or services, or being a scalable business model with a high potential of employment generation or wealth creation. This ensures eligibility for government support schemes and validates the startup's innovative nature and growth potential, which are central to the EngSUI Seed Fund's objectives. The program specifically targets startups that are leveraging technology to develop innovative solutions. While not explicitly stated, successful applicants typically demonstrate a high Technology Readiness Level (TRL) and a clear path to commercialization, aligning with the fund's objective of scaling proven technologies. The grant is primarily for Indian startups, reflecting the national focus of the Startup India initiative. DPIIT Required
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹1 Cr as seed funding with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹1 CrFund TypeSeed FundingEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableProvides up to ₹1 Crore or 100% of project cost for scaling technology-based innovations. Includes support for IP protection (patenting, trademarks), technology transfer, business model development, product refinement, and preparation for investment rounds. Offers comprehensive incubation with state-of-the-art facilities, strategic guidance, operational support, industry knowledge, and academic rigor. Support ProvidedCashIncubationMentorshipDisbursementMilestone Based
opportunities.timeline: The application process for the EngSUI Seed Fund typically involves several stages, designed to identify high-potential technology startups. Initially, interested startups are required to submit an online application through the SIIC IIT Kanpur incubation portal. This application will likely require detailed information about the startup's profile, including its DPIIT recognition…ApplicationsRolling BasisThe application process for the EngSUI Seed Fund typically involves several stages, designed to identify high-potential technology startups. This application will likely require detailed information about the startup's profile, including its DPIIT recognition status, team composition, technology description, business model, market opportunity, financial projections, and the proposed utilization of the seed fund. A comprehensive pitch deck and a detailed project proposal outlining the scaling plan are also essential submissions. Following the initial screening, shortlisted applicants may be invited for a series of interviews and presentations to a selection committee, comprising experts from SIIC IIT Kanpur, Engineers India Limited, and relevant industry domains. This stage assesses the startup's innovation, technical feasibility, market viability, team capabilities, and the potential impact of their solution. Due diligence, including verification of DPIIT recognition and other submitted documents, will also be conducted. The final selection will be based on a thorough evaluation of the proposal's alignment with the fund's objectives, the strength of the technology, the scalability of the business model, and the team's execution capabilities. Successful applicants will then proceed to formalize the funding agreement and commence their incubation journey with SIIC IIT Kanpur, receiving both financial and strategic support. Selection ProcessStarts with an online application through the SIIC IIT Kanpur portal, followed by an initial screening. Shortlisted applicants undergo interviews and presentations to a selection committee of experts. Due diligence is conducted, and final selection is based on proposal alignment, technology strength, scalability, and team capabilities.
opportunities.about: The EngSUI Seed Fund is a cornerstone of the EngSUI initiative, a strategic collaboration between Engineers India Limited (EIL) and SIIC IIT Kanpur. EIL, a prominent Public Sector Undertaking (PSU) under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, plays a vital role in India's energy and infrastructure sectors. Its commitment to this fund underscores its dedication to the Prime Minister’s 'Startup India' program, actively contributing to national development by fostering a vibrant and innovative startup ecosystem. EIL aims to leverage entrepreneurial talent to solve challenges and create new opportunities, aligning with its corporate social responsibility and national objectives. SIIC IIT Kanpur, renowned as one of India's premier technology incubators, brings invaluable expertise in identifying, nurturing, and accelerating high-potential technology ventures. It offers a comprehensive incubation environment, combining state-of-the-art facilities with deep industry knowledge and academic rigor, ensuring startups receive robust strategic guidance and operational support. This program specifically targets DPIIT-recognized startups across India that are engaged in technology-based innovations. It offers substantial financial support designed to bridge the critical gap between technological proof-of-concept and market-ready solutions. The fund aims to propel startups through the challenging 'valley of death,' enabling them to transition from validated prototypes to commercially viable products or processes. The focus is on ventures demonstrating strong potential for significant growth, market disruption, and job creation, thereby empowering them to achieve rapid commercialization and widespread market adoption. This includes support for developing a strong business model, refining product features based on market feedback, and preparing for larger investment rounds. With a maximum financial support of ₹1 Crore available per proposal, or 100% of the total project cost (whichever is lower), the fund provides crucial capital for key scaling activities. This includes critical expenses such as intellectual property (IP) protection, encompassing patenting, design registration, and trademark registry, which are vital for securing a startup's competitive advantage. Furthermore, funds can be utilized for technology transfer activities, facilitating the smooth transition of innovations from laboratory to commercial application. This comprehensive support is geared towards developing a commercially viable product or process that can achieve sustainable market traction and lead to the successful creation of a scalable enterprise.

AmountUpto ₹4. 5 CrFund TypeAcceleratorImmediate investment upon acceptance, dedicated mentorship from experienced founders/investors, peer learning in cohorts, access to an extensive alumni network, talks from successful founders, investor introductions for follow-on funding, and an in-person program at YC's San Francisco campus.
opportunities.timeline: Submit an online application through the Y Combinator application portal Applications submitted before the deadline receive a decision by June 5 Promising applications are shortlisted and invited for an interview Interviews are conducted via video conference during May and June Decisions are typically made on the same day as the… Submit an online application through the Y Combinator application portal Applications submitted before the deadline receive a decision by June 5 Promising applications are shortlisted and invited for an interview Interviews are conducted via video conference during May and June Decisions are typically made on the same day as the interview Detailed feedback is provided to all interviewed applicants Selected startups receive investment immediately upon acceptance Participation begins with a 3-day in-person kickoff followed by ongoing weekly sessions and mentorship
opportunities.about: Y Combinator Summer 2026 Batch is a globally recognized startup accelerator program designed to support early-stage startups in building and scaling high-impact companies. The program will run from July to September 2026 and will take place in person at Y Combinator’s campus in San Francisco. It is one of the most prestigious startup accelerators in the world, having backed companies such as Airbnb, Stripe, and DoorDash. The program provides startups with immediate investment upon acceptance, enabling founders to focus entirely on product development, user growth, and business model validation. Unlike traditional funding programs, Y Combinator invests as soon as a company is accepted rather than waiting for the batch to begin. This early capital infusion helps startups accelerate their progress during the program. A core component of the YC experience is mentorship. Each startup is assigned a dedicated General Partner who is an experienced founder and investor. Founders receive hands-on guidance through weekly meetings, direct communication channels, and strategic advice tailored to their specific challenges. In addition, startups are grouped into smaller cohorts, fostering deep peer learning, collaboration, and long-term relationships. The batch includes regular meetups, talks from successful founders, and access to an extensive alumni network. Founders gain exposure to insights from industry leaders and practical lessons from companies that have scaled successfully. Towards the end of the program, Y Combinator facilitates investor introductions to help startups raise follow-on funding.
