opportunities.title: BCDD Grant
opportunities.provider: Kerala Startup Mission, Backward Classes Development Department
opportunities.description: CallContactApply NowThe BCDD Funding Support Scheme, a pioneering initiative by the Government of Kerala, offers crucial early-stage financial assistance to graduates from Backward Classes. This progr
opportunities.eligibility: The applicant must be a Graduate (any stream) from Backward Classes (OBC). This criterion ensures that the program directly addresses the need for economic empowerment and entrepreneurial opportunities within these specific communities, aligning with BCDD's mandate. The applicant's age must be between 23 to 40 years. This age range targets… The applicant must be a Graduate (any stream) from Backward Classes (OBC). This age range targets individuals who have completed their education and possess some professional experience, or are ready to embark on an entrepreneurial journey with fresh perspectives, but are still in a relatively early career stage. For the Productization Grant, a minimum of 80% shareholding must be owned by OBC founder(s). This specific shareholding requirement ensures that the primary beneficiaries of the grant are indeed OBC entrepreneurs, reinforcing the program's objective of inclusive growth and wealth creation within the Backward Classes. The startup must own an innovative technology-based product or be developing one. The focus on technology ensures that the grant supports ventures with high growth potential and contributes to Kerala's evolving tech landscape. Company incorporation (Private Limited, LLP, or OPC) and a KSUM Unique ID are mandatory before fund disbursal. This ensures legal compliance, establishes the startup as a formal entity, and integrates it into the KSUM ecosystem, facilitating further support and monitoring. Only one idea can be applied for at a time; two grants will not be sanctioned simultaneously. This rule ensures fairness and focuses resources on one promising venture per applicant, preventing over-extension and maximizing the chances of success for each funded project. Minority Owned Only
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹10 Lakhs as grant with no equity dilution. AmountUpto ₹10 LakhsFund TypeGrantEquityEquity-FreeRepaymentNon-RepayableUp to ₹10 lakhs funding: an Idea Grant (₹3 lakhs for concept validation, market research) and a Productization Grant (₹7 lakhs for prototype development, pilot projects, IP, market entry, early growth). It also integrates startups into the KSUM ecosystem, providing support and monitoring. Support ProvidedCashDisbursementMilestone Based
opportunities.timeline: Applications undergo an initial screening by KSUM officials. This preliminary evaluation assesses the completeness of the application, the innovation quotient of the proposed idea, the team's background, and its alignment with the program's objectives. Shortlisted applicants are then moved to the next phase. Shortlisted applicants are invited to pitch their… Applications undergo an initial screening by KSUM officials. Shortlisted applicants are invited to pitch their ideas to an Expert Committee, which includes representatives from KSUM and BCDD officials, as well as external industry experts. This pitching session allows founders to present their business model, technology, market potential, and financial projections in detail, and to answer questions from the committee. The committee evaluates proposals based on a comprehensive set of criteria, including the strength and experience of the team, the novelty and market potential of the product innovation, the clarity and feasibility of the marketing strategy, the robustness of the financial projections, and a detailed plan for grant utilisation. This rigorous evaluation ensures that only the most promising and impactful startups receive funding. Final recommendations from the Expert Committee receive approval from the CEO/Chairman, KSUM, after a thorough review to ensure compliance with all guidelines and strategic alignment. Funds are disbursed in milestone-based tranches. This structured approach means that subsequent tranches of funding are released upon the startup achieving pre-defined milestones and submitting necessary documentation and progress reports, as outlined in an agreement signed by the recipient. This method ensures accountability and effective utilization of public funds, aligning funding with demonstrable progress. Selection ProcessInitial screening by KSUM officials. Shortlisted applicants pitch to an Expert Committee (KSUM, BCDD, industry experts). Proposals are evaluated on team, innovation, market potential, strategy, financials, and grant utilization. Final recommendations require KSUM CEO/Chairman approval. Required DocumentsCA-certified No Dues & Margin Money Certificate with UDINanyRevenue or Investment ProofanyIncorporation DocumentsanyPAN CardanyKSUM Unique IDanyBank DetailsanyBalance Sheet for Last Two YearsanyStamp PaperanyContact InformationContact:Anjali, SureshPhone:08047180470Email:[email protected]
opportunities.about: CallContactApply NowThe BCDD Funding Support Scheme, a pioneering initiative by the Government of Kerala, offers crucial early-stage financial assistance to graduates from Backward Classes. This program is designed to empower them to successfully launch and scale technology-driven startups, contributing significantly to the state's innovation landscape. Implemented collaboratively by the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM) and the Backward Classes Development Department (BCDD), the scheme actively works to foster inclusive entrepreneurship. KSUM, as the nodal agency for entrepreneurship development in Kerala, plays a vital role in building a diverse and dynamic startup ecosystem, supporting thousands of startups through various programs and initiatives, while BCDD focuses on the socio-economic upliftment of backward classes. This partnership ensures that financial support reaches deserving entrepreneurs from underrepresented communities, fostering equitable growth. The program offers structured financial support, with a maximum benefit of up to ₹10 lakhs per eligible startup. This funding is strategically divided into two distinct phases to cater to different developmental needs and milestones in a startup's journey. An initial Idea Grant provides up to ₹3 lakhs, specifically allocated for concept validation, market research, and refining foundational ideas to ensure they are viable and address a genuine problem. This initial phase helps entrepreneurs solidify their vision before committing to full-scale development. Following this, a more substantial Productization Grant offers up to ₹7 lakhs. The Productization Grant is intended to fuel critical development activities, bridging the gap between a validated concept and a market-ready product. Startups can utilize these funds for essential prototype development, conducting pilot projects, securing intellectual property, facilitating their initial market entry, and supporting early growth initiatives such as user acquisition or business development. This comprehensive financial support enables founders to transform innovative concepts into viable products and gain initial traction within their target markets, ultimately accelerating their journey towards commercialization and sustainability.

opportunities.about: The Bharat Startup Grand Challenge (BSGC) 2026 is a DPIIT-approved national startup innovation initiative aimed at identifying, supporting, and scaling high-impact startups addressing critical challenges across India’s key economic and social sectors. The program is designed to bring together startups, government bodies, industry leaders, investors, and ecosystem partners to collaboratively solve sector-specific problem statements aligned with national priorities. Through a structured challenge-led approach, startups receive financial support, mentorship, incubation opportunities, and national-level visibility. BSGC 2026 features twelve focused challenge statements spanning areas such as agriculture, healthcare, electric mobility, fintech, manufacturing, defence, sustainability, and social impact. Selected startups gain access to expert guidance, ecosystem partnerships, opportunities for pilots, and grant support to accelerate product development and real-world deployment, contributing towards the vision of a Viksit Bharat.

opportunities.about: The Vidhai Vaanam Accelerator Program Series 2 is a 12-week hybrid accelerator jointly launched by Vidhai and Vaanam Space LLP, with StartupTN serving as the ecosystem partner. Designed as a founder-first initiative, the program aims to equip early-stage startups with structured learning, deep mentoring, and strategic ecosystem access to help them scale sustainably and prepare for fundraising. Following a successful first cohort, Series 2 continues with a structured hybrid format that combines in-person weekend sessions in Chennai with weekday online sessions led by industry experts. The curriculum is intentionally built around real-world founder pain points, covering product discovery, MVP development, go-to-market strategy, branding, growth, pricing, scaling operations, financial planning, legal compliance, and AI integration. The program culminates in a Demo Day where selected founders pitch to investors and stakeholders. Apply NowParticipants benefit from 1:1 mentorship by experienced founders, operators, and investors, as well as access to domain experts and policy advisors across sectors. The accelerator emphasizes validating product-market fit, sharpening strategy, strengthening business positioning, and accelerating traction. It also prepares founders for fundraising through dedicated modules on financial forecasting, unit economics, valuation, and due diligence readiness. The program is sector-agnostic and welcomes startups from SaaS, consumer tech, hardware, deep-tech, space-tech, and other domains, provided they demonstrate intent and readiness to scale. In addition to curriculum delivery, founders gain access to a supportive peer network, ecosystem partners, tech credits, incubation support through partners, and exposure during Demo Week. By integrating structured capability building with ecosystem connectivity and investor exposure, the Vidhai Vaanam Accelerator Program is positioned as a comprehensive scale-readiness platform for ambitious early-stage founders seeking disciplined growth and long-term sustainability.

opportunities.eligibility: Open to early-stage startups. Student founders are eligible to apply. Ventures at idea to MVP stage are encouraged. Innovators seeking structured guidance and pre-incubation support may apply. Limited number of high-potential startups will be selected. Student Founder

opportunities.about: The NEEV Startup Kickstart Program – Pre-Incubation Cohort 2026 is a structured pre-incubation initiative offered by the NEEV Incubation Center under the IKN Innovation Foundation, a Section 8 company and incubation arm of IMI Kolkata. The program is designed to transform promising early-stage ideas into investment-ready ventures through a guided and ecosystem-driven development journey. ContactApply NowThis initiative focuses on nurturing early-stage startups, student founders, and innovators who are building scalable and impactful solutions. The program provides structured mentoring support through one-on-one sessions with domain experts, enabling founders to refine their business models, product strategies, and go-to-market approaches. Startups receive dedicated investment readiness support including pitch deck refinement and preparation for investor interactions. Participants gain access to investor networks and guidance on relevant government funding schemes, enhancing their ability to secure external capital. The program also offers masterclasses, shared services, legal guidance, infrastructure access, innovation labs, and prototyping facilities to help founders transition from idea to market-ready solution. The broader vision of the NEEV Incubation Center is to nurture innovation-driven and socially impactful ventures aligned with sustainable and inclusive development goals. By fostering collaboration among academia, industry, policymakers, and entrepreneurs, the program aims to strengthen India’s entrepreneurial ecosystem and create long-term socio-economic impact. Selection into the cohort is competitive, with only a limited number of high-potential startups admitted. The program emphasizes structured evaluation and careful screening to ensure that selected ventures demonstrate scalability, innovation, and impact potential.

opportunities.about: ContactApply NowStartup Srujan Seed Support (S4) is a flagship grant initiative implemented by the Gujarat Student Startup and Innovation Hub (i-Hub) under the Student Startup and Innovation Policy (SSIP 2. 0) of the Education Department, Government of Gujarat. As part of the state’s broader vision to create a holistic innovation ecosystem integrating industry, academia, and government, SSIP 2. 0 has allocated a substantial corpus to support early-stage innovators and startups across Gujarat. The S4 program is specifically designed to help young innovators transition from Proof of Concept (PoC) to product and market stage. It provides financial assistance ranging from ₹2. 5 lakh to ₹10 lakh to enable prototype development, validation, product refinement, and early commercialization activities. The initiative aims to reduce financial barriers for student innovators and early founders, empowering them to transform creative ideas into scalable ventures. Through i-Hub’s structured ecosystem support, beneficiaries gain access to mentorship, institutional backing, and innovation resources that complement the financial grant. The program covers innovators across a wide educational spectrum—from school students to doctoral scholars, alumni, and even dropouts—ensuring inclusive participation in Gujarat’s innovation movement. Startup Srujan Seed Support (S4) strengthens Gujarat’s startup landscape by fostering early-stage experimentation, validation, and venture building among youth under 35 years of age. By targeting innovators at ideation, MVP, and early stages, the program seeks to accelerate innovation-led entrepreneurship and build a strong pipeline of product-ready ventures emerging from the state’s educational and startup ecosystem.

opportunities.eligibility: Must be recognized by DPIIT under the Startup India initiative Incorporated not more than 2 years before the date of application Must have a business idea with clear market fit, viable commercialization potential, and scope for scaling Technology must be a core component of the product, service, business model, distribution… Must be recognized by DPIIT under the Startup India initiative Incorporated not more than 2 years before the date of application Must have a business idea with clear market fit, viable commercialization potential, and scope for scaling Technology must be a core component of the product, service, business model, distribution model, or methodology Must not have received more than ₹10 lakh in monetary support from any Central or State Government scheme (prize money, subsidized workspace, lab access, and founder stipends are excluded) At least 51% shareholding must be held by Indian promoters at time of application Must be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, LLP, or Registered Partnership — sole proprietors and individual applicants are not eligible Each startup can avail grant support and debt/convertible debenture support once each DPIIT Required
opportunities.benefits: Offers ₹20 Lakhs – ₹50 Lakhs as grant. Funds are milestone-based, with unsecured loans (no collateral/guarantee) at low interest, up to 5-year tenure, and 12-month moratorium. No application fees are charged at any stage.
opportunities.timeline: Obtain DPIIT recognition at startupindia. gov. in if not already done — this is mandatory before applying Log in to the SISFS portal (seedfund. startupindia. in) using your DPIIT credentials Select up to 3 participating incubators in order of preference and submit the online application — no physical submission required Applications are shared with… Obtain DPIIT recognition at startupindia. in) using your DPIIT credentials Select up to 3 participating incubators in order of preference and submit the online application — no physical submission required Applications are shared with selected incubators for eligibility check and shortlisting (Days 1–14) Shortlisted startups are evaluated by the Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC) on market need, feasibility, impact, novelty, team strength, and fund utilization plan (Days 14–30) Shortlisted startups may be called for a pitch or presentation before the ISMC (Days 30–40) Final selection communicated within 45 days; rejected applicants notified via email and may reapply after 3 months Legal agreement signed between the startup and incubator before first disbursement Grant released in milestone-based tranches; each subsequent tranche requires an interim progress report and utilization certificate Application status trackable in real-time on the Startup India portal Selection ProcessApplications are submitted to preferred incubators for eligibility checks and shortlisting. The Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC) evaluates shortlisted startups. A pitch or presentation before the ISMC may be required. Final selection is communicated within 45 days.
opportunities.about: Apply NowThe Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is a Government of India program launched by DPIIT in April 2021 with a total corpus of ₹945 crore. It provides seed-stage financial support to early-stage startups that have a validated idea or early prototype but cannot yet attract angel or venture capital investment. The scheme targets approximately 3,600 startups through a network of around 300 DPIIT-approved incubators across India. As of December 2024, over 2,600 startups have received ₹467. 75 crore in support, and more than 1,200 women-led startups have been funded. How Does SISFS Work? Funds are not disbursed directly by the government. DPIIT allocates capital to approved incubators, which then evaluate applications and release funds to selected startups. An Experts Advisory Committee (EAC) oversees incubator selection and scheme implementation. Each incubator constitutes an Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC) — comprising domain experts, VC and angel investor representatives, state government nominees, and experienced entrepreneurs — to evaluate and select startups for funding. How Much Funding Can a Startup Get? Support TypeAmountPurposeGrantUp to ₹20 lakhProof of concept, prototype development, product trialsDebt / Convertible DebenturesUp to ₹50 lakhMarket entry, commercialization, scaling Grant funds are released in milestone-based tranches. Debt instruments carry interest at no more than the prevailing RBI repo rate, a tenure of up to 5 years, and a moratorium of up to 12 months. Loans are unsecured — no collateral or personal guarantee required. Funds go directly to the startup's bank account. A startup can avail both the grant and the debt support — once each — under the scheme. Who Should Apply? SISFS is for tech-driven startups at idea or MVP stage that are DPIIT-recognized and less than 2 years old at the time of application. The scheme is sector-agnostic, but incubators give preference to startups in healthcare, agriculture, biotech, social impact, clean energy, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, mobility, defense, and space. Documents Required DPIIT recognition certificate Certificate of incorporation PAN card and Aadhaar of founders Shareholding pattern / cap table showing ≥51% Indian promoter holding Business plan or pitch deck Prototype details or proof of concept documentation Financial projections and fund utilization plan Bank account details (cancelled cheque or statement) Frequently Asked Questions Can individual entrepreneurs apply for SISFS? No. Only DPIIT-recognized entities incorporated as Private Limited Companies, LLPs, or Registered Partnerships are eligible. Sole proprietors and individuals cannot apply. How many incubators can I apply to? You can apply to up to 3 participating incubators simultaneously. If more than one selects you, funding comes from your highest-preference incubator that selects you. Is DPIIT recognition mandatory? Yes. Your startup must be recognized under Startup India before applying. You can get recognition at startupindia. gov. in. Is there any application fee? Zero fees at every stage — application, selection, disbursement, and monitoring. Incubators are prohibited from charging any fees to applicants or beneficiaries. What if my startup has already received government funding? You are ineligible if you have received more than ₹10 lakh in monetary support from any Central or State Government scheme. Prize money, subsidized workspace, lab access, and monthly founder allowances are excluded from this limit. How long does selection take? The ISMC selects startups within 45 days of receiving the application. Can I reapply after a rejection? You can reapply after 3 months from the date of rejection. What can the funds not be used for? Seed funds cannot be used to create physical facilities or infrastructure. They must be used strictly for the purpose stated in the agreement — R&D, product development, trials, or market entry. What happens if the startup fails? The entrepreneur submits a report covering learnings and reasons for failure, along with an audited utilization certificate. Loans are unsecured, so no personal liability applies beyond the incubator agreement terms.
opportunities.raw_scraped_text: StartupGrants IndiaGrantsEventsCompetitionsOffersSearch…⌘KSign upSign upLoading...Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) For StartupsFunding amountEligibilityBenefitsTimelinesDepartment for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) For StartupsFunding amountGovt seed grant up to ₹20L + ₹50L debt for DPIIT-recognised Indian tech startups. Rolling applicationsStageIdea, MVPTypeGrantLocationPan IndiaApply NowApply NowAbout this grantThe Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is a Government of India program launched by DPIIT in April 2021 with a total corpus of ₹945 crore. It provides seed-stage financial support to early-stage startups that have a validated idea or early prototype but cannot yet attract angel or venture capital investment. The scheme targets approximately 3,600 startups through a network of around 300 DPIIT-approved incubators across India. As of December 2024, over 2,600 startups have received ₹467.75 crore in support, and more than 1,200 women-led startups have been funded. How Does SISFS Work? Funds are not disbursed directly by the government. DPIIT allocates capital to approved incubators, which then evaluate applications and release funds to selected startups. An Experts Advisory Committee (EAC) oversees incubator selection and scheme implementation. Each incubator constitutes an Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC) — comprising domain experts, VC and angel investor representatives, state government nominees, and experienced entrepreneurs — to evaluate and select startups for funding. How Much Funding Can a Startup Get? Support TypeAmountPurposeGrantUp to ₹20 lakhProof of concept, prototype development, product trialsDebt / Convertible DebenturesUp to ₹50 lakhMarket entry, commercialization, scaling Grant funds are released in milestone-based tranches. Debt instruments carry interest at no more than the prevailing RBI repo rate, a tenure of up to 5 years, and a moratorium of up to 12 months. Loans are unsecured — no collateral or personal guarantee required. Funds go directly to the startup's bank account. A startup can avail both the grant and the debt support — once each — under the scheme. Who Should Apply? SISFS is for tech-driven startups at idea or MVP stage that are DPIIT-recognized and less than 2 years old at the time of application. The scheme is sector-agnostic, but incubators give preference to startups in healthcare, agriculture, biotech, social impact, clean energy, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, mobility, defense, and space. Documents Required DPIIT recognition certificate Certificate of incorporation PAN card and Aadhaar of founders Shareholding pattern / cap table showing ≥51% Indian promoter holding Business plan or pitch deck Prototype details or proof of concept documentation Financial projections and fund utilization plan Bank account details (cancelled cheque or statement) Frequently Asked Questions Can individual entrepreneurs apply for SISFS? No. Only DPIIT-recognized entities incorporated as Private Limited Companies, LLPs, or Registered Partnerships are eligible. Sole proprietors and individuals cannot apply. How many incubators can I apply to? You can apply to up to 3 participating incubators simultaneously. If more than one selects you, funding comes from your highest-preference incubator that selects you. Is DPIIT recognition mandatory? Yes. Your startup must be recognized under Startup India before applying. You can get recognition at startupindia.gov.in. Is there any application fee? No. Zero fees at every stage — application, selection, disbursement, and monitoring. Incubators are prohibited from charging any fees to applicants or beneficiaries. What if my startup has already received government funding? You are ineligible if you have received more than ₹10 lakh in monetary support from any Central or State Government scheme. Prize money, subsidized workspace, lab access, and monthly founder allowances are excluded from this limit. How long does selection take? The ISMC selects startups within 45 days of receiving the application. Can I reapply after a rejection? Yes. You can reapply after 3 months from the date of rejection. What can the funds not be used for? Seed funds cannot be used to create physical facilities or infrastructure. They must be used strictly for the purpose stated in the agreement — R&D, product development, trials, or market entry. What happens if the startup fails? The entrepreneur submits a report covering learnings and reasons for failure, along with an audited utilization certificate. Loans are unsecured, so no personal liability applies beyond the incubator agreement terms.ProviderDepartment for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of IndiaDPIIT is a department under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India, established in 1995 and renamed from DIPP in 2019. It is responsible for the Startup India initiative — India's flagship national startup program launched in 2016 — and has recognized over 1,97,692 startups across the country as of…Government • Est. opportunities.title: WE-Engage SPARK Cohort 3
opportunities.description: WE Hub, India's first state-led nodal organisation for women entrepreneurs, was established by the Government of Telangana. It presents the WE-Engage SPARK Cohort 3, a 12-week intensive accelerator pr
opportunities.eligibility: Women-led startups with a minimum of 51% female ownership in the company are eligible to apply; the majority stake and leadership must be held by a woman founder or co-founder. Applicants must have a validated MVP (Minimum Viable Product) with demonstrated early traction or early revenue generation, confirming the startup… Women-led startups with a minimum of 51% female ownership in the company are eligible to apply; the majority stake and leadership must be held by a woman founder or co-founder. Applicants must have a validated MVP (Minimum Viable Product) with demonstrated early traction or early revenue generation, confirming the startup has moved beyond ideation and pure development into market validation. The business must be legally registered and currently operating in India; unregistered ventures or those operating outside India are not eligible. The program is designed specifically for founders ready to scale beyond their initial traction toward sustainable market growth — not startups still in the process of building their core product or testing basic product-market fit. Startups from any sector are considered, provided they are women-led and meet the ownership, registration, and traction criteria described above. Applicants are expected to commit to the full 12-week intensive program, which includes bootcamp sessions, one-on-one mentoring engagements, and investor interaction activities organised by WE Hub. Commitment to all scheduled program activities and engagements is mandatory for selection. Women-Led Only

Fund TypeAcceleratorA 12-week intensive accelerator program offering personalised mentorship from industry experts and hands-on bootcamp training for market entry and scaling. opportunities.about: WE Hub, India's first state-led nodal organisation for women entrepreneurs, was established by the Government of Telangana. It presents the WE-Engage SPARK Cohort 3, a 12-week intensive accelerator program. This initiative targets early-stage women-led startups that have validated their Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and demonstrated early market traction or initial revenue. The program aims to help founders scale effectively beyond initial successes and establish sustainable market growth for their ventures. The accelerator delivers personalised mentorship from industry experts, providing guidance for navigating business expansion. Participants engage in hands-on bootcamp training sessions, focused on practical strategies for market entry and scaling techniques. A key benefit includes access to diverse funding opportunities, encompassing grants, seed capital, and debt financing, alongside networking connections to investors and key ecosystem partners essential for sustained growth.

opportunities.description: The GRO8 AI-Enabled Venture Accelerator is a 12-week program by 888VC, designed to integrate AI into daily startup operations across strategy, sales, finance, product, and fundraising. It aims to prov
opportunities.eligibility: The program specifically targets pre-seed and seed-stage startups that have moved beyond the ideation phase and are actively building and selling their product. Eligible ventures must have achieved early market validation, with evidence of initial customer traction, revenue, or strong product-market fit signals. Applicants are required to demonstrate clear growth… The program specifically targets pre-seed and seed-stage startups that have moved beyond the ideation phase and are actively building and selling their product. Applicants are required to demonstrate clear growth momentum, showing measurable progress in user acquisition, revenue, or partnerships. A functional product must be available in the market, supported by a well-defined go-to-market strategy and early customer feedback. Startups should be technology-enabled, leveraging innovation as a core component of their business model and product offering. The program seeks companies with an inherent global ambition, designed to scale across markets beyond their home country from the very beginning. Founders are expected to commit fully to the 12-week program, actively participating in mentoring sessions, workshops, and investor engagement activities. The accelerator focuses on sectors including Deep Tech, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Consumer, Healthtech, Sustainability, Manufacturing, Fintech, and SaaS.
opportunities.benefits: Offers ₹21 Lakhs – ₹4. 2 Cr as accelerator. Amount₹21 Lakhs – ₹4. 2 CrFund TypeAcceleratorParticipants receive a minimum $25,000 direct investment, potential $50,000-$100,000 for standout companies, and access to seed funding up to $500,000. Benefits include 12-week hands-on mentoring, GTM strategy, investor readiness, AI automation, pitch reviews, a $60,000 startup kit, and extensive connections to over 200 VCs, 1000+ angels, CFOs, CXOs, and family offices for accelerated growth and investor access.

opportunities.about: The GRO8 AI-Enabled Venture Accelerator is a 12-week program by 888VC, designed to integrate AI into daily startup operations across strategy, sales, finance, product, and fundraising. It aims to provide execution-focused support, moving beyond mere ideation for participating ventures. Each cohort consists of 12 startups, benefiting from hands-on mentoring and practical operator knowledge. Participants receive a minimum $25,000 direct investment, with potential for $50,000-$100,000 for standout companies, and access to seed funding up to $500,000. Key support areas include GTM strategy development, investor readiness, AI automation implementation, and pitch reviews. Startups also gain a startup kit valued at $60,000 USD, along with extensive connections to a network of over 200 Seed/early stage VC funds, 1000+ angels, CFOs, and CXOs in India, and 100+ CXO/CIOs from the US, plus 50+ connected family offices. This comprehensive support facilitates accelerated growth and investor access.

opportunities.title: Seed Fund Scheme - Kerala Startup Mission
opportunities.description: Seed funding (loan) for early-stage Kerala startups working on innovative tech products.
opportunities.eligibility: Applicants must meet the following criteria: The startup must be registered in Kerala as an LLP or Private Limited Company. This ensures the program directly benefits the state's economy and integrates the startup into the local regulatory framework. The startup must have an active KSUM Unique ID, signifying active participation…Applicants must meet the following criteria: The startup must be registered in Kerala as an LLP or Private Limited Company. The startup must have an active KSUM Unique ID, signifying active participation in the state's startup ecosystem and engagement with KSUM's support initiatives. The startup company must have DIPP registration and an 'Active'/'Active Compliant' status with MCA. This confirms the startup's legitimacy and compliance with national startup policies, crucial for accessing government support. The startup must be working on an innovative product or technology. The scheme is specifically designed to foster technological advancements and new solutions, rather than incremental improvements or traditional services. The scheme prioritizes product/technology startups and typically excludes service startups and SMEs in trade and commerce. This clear focus ensures funds are directed towards ventures with high growth potential and innovation. The startup shall not have any other pending dues with any Government agencies, KSUM, or other incubators in the state, ensuring financial good standing. The startup shall not be blacklisted by any Government agency in India, confirming a clean track record. Startup directors should have a CIBIL Score preferably more than 750, as this is a loan program and a good credit score reflects financial responsibility and repayment capability. The startup should generally be at the prototype/MVP stage, indicating that the product has moved beyond the ideation phase and has a tangible, testable version. DPIIT Required
opportunities.benefits: Offers up to ₹15 Lakhs as seed funding. AmountUpto ₹15 LakhsFund TypeSeed FundingRepaymentRepayableSeed funding up to Rs. 15 lakhs as a 6% soft loan, with a 12-month moratorium. Funds support product/customer development, marketing, scaling, testing, consultancy, IPR, and manpower. Benefits also include potential access to insights, strategic guidance, and mentorship through KSUM representatives and expert committees. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipDisbursementTranched
opportunities.timeline: The application process for the Seed Fund Scheme involves several key stages: Submit the comprehensive online seed application form, which includes a detailed Business Profile and a compelling Seed Proposal, via the KSUM website. This initial submission is crucial for showcasing the startup's vision, market potential, and financial projections. Engage…Avg. Processing30 daysApplicationsRolling BasisThe application process for the Seed Fund Scheme involves several key stages: Submit the comprehensive online seed application form, which includes a detailed Business Profile and a compelling Seed Proposal, via the KSUM website. Engage in an initial evaluation call and pitch with KSUM representatives. This stage allows KSUM to assess the basic viability and alignment of the startup with the scheme's objectives, providing an opportunity for the founders to articulate their project directly. Present a final, in-depth pitch to the Seed Expert Committee, comprising industry leaders and subject matter experts. This committee rigorously evaluates the innovation, business model, team capability, and potential impact, ultimately shortlisting the most promising applicants for funding. Selected startups then proceed to provide necessary legal, financial, and operational documents, and sign formal agreements for installment-based fund disbursal. This includes submitting detailed utilization plans, progress reports, and other compliance-related paperwork to ensure transparent and accountable use of funds. Selection ProcessApplicants submit an online form, followed by an initial evaluation call and pitch with KSUM representatives. A final, in-depth pitch is then made to the Seed Expert Committee for rigorous evaluation and shortlisting. opportunities.about: The Government of Kerala introduced the Seed Support Scheme to provide crucial financial assistance to early-stage startups, fostering their growth into innovative, technology-driven businesses. This scheme, managed by the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM), aims to promote new ventures, create jobs, and attract private investment, contributing significantly to the state's economic development. KSUM, as a state government entity, plays a pivotal role in nurturing a thriving startup ecosystem within Kerala, offering various programs and infrastructure support for emerging businesses. Under this scheme, eligible startups can receive seed funding up to Rs. 15 lakhs. This financial aid is provided as a soft loan against purchase orders, with an interest rate of 6% simple interest per annum, making it an accessible option for nascent ventures. The funds are carefully disbursed in tranches, with an initial release followed by subsequent installments upon submission of detailed progress reports and utilization certificates, ensuring accountability and alignment with project milestones. A generous 12-month moratorium is available before repayment begins in 36 convenient monthly installments, offering startups critical breathing room during their most vulnerable growth phase. Prepayment is permitted without penalty, providing flexibility to successful ventures. The seed fund is specifically intended to support critical growth activities essential for bringing innovative products to market. These include intense product development, crucial customer development initiatives to validate market fit, targeted marketing efforts, and strategic scaling up of operations. Funds can also cover essential expenses for rigorous testing and trials, engaging professional consultancy for specialized expertise, and addressing intellectual property rights (IPR) issues to secure innovations. Manpower costs for day-to-day operations are also eligible, ensuring startups can maintain essential functions and retain talent during their early, resource-intensive development period. Beyond monetary support, the scheme offers indirect yet invaluable benefits. KSUM's central role in the state's startup ecosystem means that applicants engage with experienced representatives and expert committees throughout the evaluation and funding process. This engagement provides potential access to valuable insights, strategic guidance, and mentorship that extends beyond the financial transaction. The program's unwavering focus on innovative products is designed to cultivate a high-tech startup landscape in Kerala, positioning the state as a hub for technological advancement and entrepreneurial success.

This ensures the program directly benefits the state's economy and integrates the startup into the local regulatory framework. The startup must have an active KSUM Unique ID, signifying active participation…Applicants must meet the following criteria: The startup must be registered in Kerala as an LLP or Private Limited Company. This ensures the program directly benefits the state's economy and integrates the startup into the local regulatory framework. The startup must have an active KSUM Unique ID, signifying active participation in the state's startup ecosystem and engagement with KSUM's support initiatives. The startup company must have DIPP registration and an 'Active'/'Active Compliant' status with MCA. This confirms the startup's legitimacy and compliance with national startup policies, crucial for accessing government support. The startup must be working on an innovative product or technology. The scheme is specifically designed to foster technological advancements and new solutions, rather than incremental improvements or traditional services. The scheme prioritizes product/technology startups and typically excludes service startups and SMEs in trade and commerce. This clear focus ensures funds are directed towards ventures with high growth potential and innovation. The startup shall not have any other pending dues with any Government agencies, KSUM, or other incubators in the state, ensuring financial good standing. The startup shall not be blacklisted by any Government agency in India, confirming a clean track record. Startup directors should have a CIBIL Score preferably more than 750, as this is a loan program and a good credit score reflects financial responsibility and repayment capability. The startup should generally be at the prototype/MVP stage, indicating that the product has moved beyond the ideation phase and has a tangible, testable version. opportunities.description: EWOR invests in individuals at any stage, from idea to €500K ARR, fostering potential.
opportunities.eligibility: The program focuses on investing in individuals, rather than strictly requiring an established company entity for application. Applicants are welcomed at any entrepreneurial stage, ranging from those who do not yet have a developed business idea to those generating up to €500K in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR). There are no…The program focuses on investing in individuals, rather than strictly requiring an established company entity for application. There are no specific minimum or maximum revenue generation thresholds that serve as barriers to entry for applicants. Prior Funding Allowed
opportunities.benefits: as seed funding. Fund TypeSeed FundingEWOR provides financial investment (capital), foundational support, and resources. Benefits include comprehensive personal and professional backing, continuous support, and integration into the EWOR ecosystem. This offers opportunities to connect with EWOR Mentors, engage with EWOR Fellows, join a broader talent pool, and receive crucial guidance and networking. Support ProvidedCashMentorshipNetworking
opportunities.timeline: ApplicationsRolling Basis
opportunities.about: EWOR is currently accepting applications for its program, designed for individuals passionate about entrepreneurship. This initiative underscores a fundamental philosophy: EWOR primarily invests in people and their potential, rather than strictly focusing on existing companies. The organization aims to cultivate a community of driven individuals, providing them with the necessary resources and environment to thrive. This unique approach allows for broad inclusivity, ensuring that innovative minds are supported at various stages of their entrepreneurial development. EWOR is an organization dedicated to fostering the next generation of entrepreneurs by focusing on individual potential rather than just existing ventures. With a strong belief that great founders can emerge from any background and at any stage, EWOR has built a reputation for its founder-first approach. Its mission is to identify, nurture, and empower ambitious individuals, providing them with the foundational support, capital, and network needed to transform their ideas into successful companies. This commitment is reflected in its diverse portfolio of supported individuals and ventures across various sectors. A cornerstone of the EWOR program is its adaptable criteria regarding the applicant's current business standing or revenue. Whether an individual is in the initial phases, still formulating a concept without a definitive business idea, or has progressed to generating up to €500K in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR), their application is considered with equal merit. This flexibility is a deliberate choice, reflecting EWOR's commitment to unlocking potential irrespective of prior commercial traction. It eliminates conventional barriers often found in funding programs, allowing for a more diverse pool of applicants. Beyond financial investment, EWOR emphasizes comprehensive personal and professional backing. The program is structured to invest in and provide continuous support to selected individuals. This support extends to integrating applicants into the wider EWOR ecosystem, which includes opportunities to connect with EWOR Mentors, engage with other EWOR Fellows, and potentially be part of a broader talent pool. This framework is designed to empower individuals, offering guidance and networking crucial for their journey from nascent ideas to impactful ventures.
